entries.title: Eri Is Under U.A. Care After The Raid

context.label: Eri is placed under U.A. care and Aizawa monitoring

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After the Shie Hassaikai raid, Eri is placed under U.A. supervision because she cannot safely return to ordinary family care.
content.injection: Treat Eri as protected by U.A. staff and monitored by Aizawa. Her safety and Quirk control are institutional responsibilities, not casual dorm errands.
constraints: Eri is rescued but not emotionally or medically resolved.
Aizawa’s Erasure is important because Rewind remains dangerous.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Eri Recovery Is Fragile Not Finished

context.label: Eri is placed under U.A. care and Aizawa monitoring

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Eri’s rescue does not erase her trauma, fear, isolation, or limited social experience.
content.injection: Write Eri as sheltered, wary, and slowly learning safety. Do not treat the festival as instant healing or a full personality reset.

content.fact: Mirio remains emotionally important to Eri after losing Permeation, even while he cannot currently operate as Lemillion.
content.injection: Keep Mirio warm, present, and protective around Eri. His lost Quirk must not erase his role as someone she trusts.

entries.title: Aizawa Is Eri Safety Control Not Foster Parent

context.label: Eri is placed under U.A. care and Aizawa monitoring

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aizawa’s role with Eri is primarily safety monitoring and school responsibility because Erasure can suppress Rewind if needed.
content.injection: Use Aizawa as a practical safety authority for Eri’s Quirk. Do not recast him as emotionally expressive caretaker unless the user story diverges.

entries.title: Class 1-A Wants To Repay The School Mood

context.label: U.A. School Festival preparations begin under public doubt

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Class 1-A wants the School Festival performance to relieve tension among students who suffered because of the hero course’s crises.
content.injection: Frame the festival performance as morale repair. Class 1-A is not just seeking applause; they are trying to answer resentment and fear with shared relief.

entries.title: Non-Hero Students Resent Hero Course Disruptions

context.label: U.A. School Festival preparations begin under public doubt

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Some non-hero U.A. students resent how repeated villain incidents around Class 1-A have disrupted ordinary school life.
content.injection: Let non-hero students have valid frustration. Do not make the whole school automatically admire Class 1-A just because they survive villain attacks.

content.fact: U.A. holds the School Festival under tighter security because the school has already suffered villain attacks and public scrutiny.
content.injection: Keep festival scenes under security pressure. Visitors, schedules, and disruptions matter because U.A. cannot afford another breach.

entries.title: Class 1-A Chooses Performance Over Combat Display

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Class 1-A chooses an entertainment performance rather than turning the festival into another demonstration of combat power.
content.injection: The class’s plan should feel intentionally nonviolent: music, dancing, staging, and spectacle become their way to support others.

entries.title: Jiro Is Reluctant But Central To The Performance

context.label: Jiro becomes central to the class performance

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Jiro’s musical skill becomes central to the class performance, even though she is initially self-conscious about exposing that side of herself.
content.injection: When Jiro is involved in festival prep, emphasize reluctant vulnerability and competence. Her music matters because classmates value a private passion.

entries.title: Eri Does Not Understand Ordinary Festival Joy Yet

context.label: Eri is placed under U.A. care and Aizawa monitoring

content.fact: Eri has little experience with ordinary childhood joy, school festivals, or safe excitement.
content.injection: In Eri scenes before the performance, let simple school customs be unfamiliar. Her reactions should be cautious, curious, and uncertain.

entries.title: The Festival Is A Civilian Trust Exercise

context.label: U.A. School Festival preparations begin under public doubt

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The School Festival tests whether U.A. can create ordinary student life under public suspicion after repeated hero-course crises.
content.injection: Treat the festival as a trust exercise for the school, not filler. A disruption would damage U.A.’s legitimacy and student morale.

entries.title: Performance Prep Shows Class 1-A As More Than Fighters

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The preparation arc shows Class 1-A using creativity, teamwork, and care rather than only combat skill.
content.injection: When Class 1-A prepares, write their heroism through work ethic, talent sharing, and morale repair, not only Quirk drills.

entries.title: School Festival Should Not Leak Later War Stakes

context.label: U.A. School Festival preparations begin under public doubt

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: During the School Festival period, Class 1-A does not know the later PLF war, traitor reveal, Dark Hero crisis, or Final War outcomes.
content.injection: Keep School Festival scenes locally bounded. Do not let students speak with later-war knowledge unless the user has loaded an AU divergence.
